text: Here we present a case of a sixty-year-old patient with symptomatic grade IV hemorrhoids who underwent closed hemorrhoidectomy.
The patient is positioned in a prone jack-knife position on the operating table, with the hips elevated and knees slightly bent to facilitate access to the rectal area.
The surgeon performs a dorsal perineal block by injecting a mixture of lidocaine and bupivacaine into three different directions at each site, preparing the area for anesthesia.
The surgeon inserts an Onsler anoscope into the anal canal, providing a clear view of the rectal area for the procedure.
The surgeon uses forceps to retract the hemorrhoidal tissue at the three o'clock position into the lumen, performing a V-shaped excision extending up to two centimeters above the dentate line.
The surgeon applies continuous sutures to ligate the vascular pedicle, extending the suturing up to the perineal skin.
The surgeon repeats the v-shaped excision at the eleven o'clock position, retracting the pile into the lumen with forceps and excising up to two centimeters above the dentate line.
The surgeon performs coagulation and suturing of the pectin at the seven o'clock position, using precise techniques to manage tissue in the rectal area.
A hemostatic sponge is placed intra-anally using the anoscope.
